breaking_changes.title: PHP 8.1 minimum requirement
breaking_changes.description: Laravel 10 requires PHP 8.1 or higher. Projects running PHP 8.0 must upgrade PHP before applying this hop.

breaking_changes.title: Native return types added to all framework classes
breaking_changes.description: Laravel 10 adds native PHP return types to ALL framework classes. Any application class that extends a framework class and overrides a method WITHOUT a return type declaration will cause a PHP deprecation notice or fatal error. This is the highest-volume change in the L9→L10 upgrade.

breaking_changes.title: assertDeleted() removed — use assertModelMissing()
breaking_changes.description: The assertDeleted() method was deprecated in Laravel 9 and removed in Laravel 10. Use assertModelMissing() for model-based assertions, or assertDatabaseMissing() for table-based assertions.

breaking_changes.title: Bus::dispatchNow() and $this->dispatchNow() removed
breaking_changes.description: The dispatchNow() method was deprecated in Laravel 8 and removed in Laravel 10. Use Bus::dispatchSync() or $this->dispatchSync() instead.

breaking_changes.title: Model $dates property fully removed
breaking_changes.description: The deprecated $dates property on Eloquent models is fully removed in Laravel 10. Use the $casts property with 'datetime' cast. This was deprecated in Laravel 9 but still functional; now it is gone.

breaking_changes.title: RouteServiceProvider $namespace property removed
breaking_changes.description: The $namespace property in RouteServiceProvider has been fully removed. Routes must use fully qualified class names or the use statement import. Applications still relying on App\Http\Controllers auto-prefix must update all route files.

breaking_changes.title: Deprecated helper functions removed (str, array helpers)
breaking_changes.description: Various deprecated helper functions removed in Laravel 10 including some string and array helpers that were moved to Str and Arr facades in Laravel 9.

breaking_changes.title: Doctrine DBAL no longer required for column modifications
breaking_changes.description: Laravel 10 no longer requires doctrine/dbal for column modifications in migrations. The framework now uses its own schema grammar for column changes. Projects can remove the doctrine/dbal dependency unless they use DBAL-specific features directly.

migration_example.after: // doctrine/dbal can be removed from composer.json if not used directly

breaking_changes.title: ServiceProvider::boot() return type added
breaking_changes.description: The boot() method on ServiceProvider now has a void return type. Custom service providers must update their boot() method signatures to include : void.

breaking_changes.title: Middleware handle() method return type added
breaking_changes.description: Middleware handle() methods in Laravel 10 now have a return type of Response. Custom middleware must add the return type to avoid deprecation notices.

migration_example.after: public function handle(Request $request, Closure $next): Response { return $next($request); }

breaking_changes.title: Minimum dependency version bumps
breaking_changes.description: Laravel 10 bumps minimum versions of several first-party packages. composer update will handle most of these, but locked constraints may reject the upgrade.

breaking_changes.title: Exception handler method return types added
breaking_changes.description: The render(), report(), shouldReport(), and register() methods on the exception handler now have return types. Custom exception handlers must update their signatures.

breaking_changes.title: Password validation rule defaults changed
breaking_changes.description: The Password::defaults() method in Laravel 10 now requires at least 8 characters by default and includes additional rules. Applications relying on the previous defaults may need to explicitly configure password rules.

breaking_changes.title: Closure-based fakes for Queue/Bus/Mail interactions updated
breaking_changes.description: Testing assertions for queued closures have changed behaviour. assertPushed and assertDispatched now handle closure jobs differently.

migration_example.after: // Review closure-based queue assertions — may need updating

breaking_changes.title: Redis cache tag flushing behaviour changed
breaking_changes.description: Cache::tags()->flush() on Redis now uses SCAN-based deletion instead of a single DEL. This is more memory-efficient but changes the atomicity behaviour.

migration_example.after: // Behaviour unchanged in API, but underlying implementation changed. Review if atomicity matters.
